definition:
1. heavy-armed, armed , δρόμος ὁπλ. a race of men in armour , opposite to the naked  race, (Pindar); ὁπλ. στρατός an armed  host, (Euripides); ὁπλ. κόσμος warrior- dress, armour, (Euripides) 
2. as Substantive, a heavy-armed foot-soldier, man-at-arms , who carried a large shield  ὅπλον); whence the name, as the light-armed foot-soldier  πελταστής); had his from the light πέλτη, (Herdotus Historicus), attic; ὁπλῖται are opposite to ψιλοί, (Herdotus Historicus) (ML)
